paperTitle: Mario: Multimodal Graph Reasoning with LLMs
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "Mario: Multimodal Graph Reasoning with LLMs".

Key contribution: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for multimodal reasoning. Yet, most existing methods still rely on pretrained vision-language models (VLMs) to encode image-text pairs in isolation, ignoring the relational structure that real-world multimodal data naturally form.

## When to use

- Apply when solving mario problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05181
